summary: Great, clean audience recording of a late‑90s show highlighted by a stunning Liberty and an unforgettable Crazy Fingers solo.
review: The consensus among reviewers is that this late‑90s Oakland concert captures the band at a creative peak, with three new songs, a breathtaking Liberty performance and Jerry Garcia’s legendary Crazy Fingers solo.  The audience (FOB) tape is described as very good – clear enough to hear crowd reactions and acoustic nuances – though a few listeners note occasional muddiness.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating of 4.36 combined with overwhelmingly positive comments about the performance outweighs the few criticisms of a sloppy set, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners praise the clarity and balance of the FOB tape, noting crisp crowd noise and clean acoustic detail; a couple mention intermittent bits but no major drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans highlight the excitement of three brand‑new songs, a relaxed yet engaging second set and a reverent atmosphere that kept the pacing smooth throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for his acoustic‑MIDI work, beautiful tone on Liberty and an iconic solo in Crazy Fingers.
